title: Dysuria / Possible Pyelonephritis Knowledge Pack Example
review_notes: Demonstration pack using public guideline sources and de-identified generic clinical content. No raw chart text or patient identifiers are included.

source_registry.license_or_access_notes: Public IDSA guideline page; use according to site terms and local citation policy.
source_registry.preferred_citation: Infectious Diseases Society of America. 2025 Guideline Update on Complicated Urinary Tract Infections.

applicability.sex_or_reproductive_context: adult dysuria patient; pregnancy status must be assessed when medication, imaging, or disposition safety could change
use_when: Use for adult dysuria, urinary frequency, flank pain, fever, or possible pyelonephritis workups.
do_not_use_when: Do not use as a pediatric UTI workup or as pregnancy-specific obstetric care without separate reviewed guidance.

items.label: Ask about flank pain and fever
items.text: Any flank pain, fever, rigors, pregnancy, vomiting, or systemic symptoms with dysuria?
items.options: No / Flank pain / Fever or chills / Both / Other ___
detail_prompts: Ask specifically about flank pain or CVA-area pain.
Ask specifically about fever, chills, or rigors.
Ask about vomiting, poor intake, or systemic illness.
items.when_to_ask: Ask when dysuria, urinary frequency, flank pain, fever, or possible urinary infection is the selected staged intent.
items.diagnostic_purpose: Screens for upper urinary tract involvement, systemic infection, pregnancy-specific risk, or complicated UTI features.
items.management_implication: A positive answer changes uncomplicated-cystitis framing, urgency, testing, pregnancy safety, imaging threshold, and antibiotic/disposition planning.
items.diagnostic_target: Upper urinary tract involvement or systemic infection
items.result_changes_management: Fever, rigors, CVA pain, or systemic symptoms should prompt escalation beyond uncomplicated cystitis framing.
items.likelihood_ratio_note: Question-level LR+/LR- is not available unless the cited evidence validates the exact response; use this answer to localize urinary source risk and guide testing/escalation.
items.reviewer_notes: Source-backed demonstration item for staging workflow.

items.label: Verify pregnancy possibility
items.action: Ask or verify pregnancy possibility before choosing antibiotics, imaging, or disposition for dysuria with possible upper-tract infection.
items.rationale: Pregnancy status is not a diagnostic maneuver but changes medication safety, imaging decisions, obstetric involvement, and admission threshold.
items.diagnostic_target: Pregnancy-associated urinary infection risk and medication/imaging safety
items.result_changes_management: Pregnancy possibility changes urine culture threshold, antibiotic choice, imaging safety, obstetric involvement, and disposition.
items.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item for medication, imaging, and disposition safety.
items.equipment_needed: privacy-aware history; urine pregnancy test when clinically appropriate
items.limitations: Use privacy-aware wording and interpret alongside test availability, gestational context, ectopic symptoms, and local policy.
items.reviewer_notes: Source-backed demonstration safety-check item for staging workflow.

items.label: CVA tenderness
items.technique: Percuss or palpate over each costovertebral angle and compare for reproducible flank pain.
items.options: Absent / Present right / Present left / Present bilateral / Unable
items.condition_or_syndrome: Dysuria; flank pain; possible pyelonephritis or nephrolithiasis
items.result_changes_management: CVA tenderness with fever or systemic illness can support pyelonephritis/stone complication framing and urgency of workup.
items.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat CVA tenderness as supportive and interpret with fever, urinalysis, pregnancy status, and pain pattern.
items.limitations: Absence does not exclude pyelonephritis or renal colic; interpret with fever, urinalysis, pregnancy status, pain pattern, and systemic illness.
items.reviewer_notes: Source-backed demonstration exam item for staging workflow.

items.label: Urinalysis with urine culture when complicated infection features are present
items.action: Order urinalysis and culture when systemic illness, pregnancy, pyelonephritis concern, treatment failure, or complicated UTI features are present.
items.diagnostic_target: Evidence of urinary inflammation, bacteriuria, hematuria, or complicated infection features
items.result_changes_management: Positive infectious or hematuria findings change antibiotic selection, culture follow-up, imaging threshold, and uncomplicated-cystitis framing.
items.likelihood_ratio_note: Test-level LR+/LR- depends on the exact urinalysis component, threshold, population, and culture definition; use local lab performance and the cited guideline context.
items.diagnostic_thresholds: Interpret urinalysis and culture using local laboratory reporting and symptom context; pyuria, nitrite/leukocyte esterase positivity, hematuria, or culture growth can support urinary-source evaluation when clinically consistent.
items.interpretation_cautions: Contamination, asymptomatic bacteriuria, early infection, prior antibiotics, pregnancy, catheter status, and local resistance patterns can change interpretation and treatment decisions.
items.reviewer_notes: Source-backed demonstration diagnostic-test item for staging workflow.

items.label: Renal function threshold for antibiotic dosing and imaging safety
items.action: Interpret creatinine and eGFR before selecting renally cleared antibiotics, contrast imaging, or disposition for dysuria with systemic or flank symptoms.
items.diagnostic_target: Renal impairment affecting antibiotic dosing, contrast safety, AKI framing, or disposition
items.when_to_use_structured: dysuria; pyelonephritis; flank_pain; AKI; renal_function; antibiotic_dosing
items.result_changes_management: Reduced eGFR, rising creatinine, oliguria, or AKI concern changes renal dosing, nephrotoxin avoidance, contrast-imaging planning, AKI workup, and disposition.
items.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to a renal-function safety threshold; interpret the value against baseline kidney function and local laboratory reporting.
items.reference_ranges: Use local laboratory adult creatinine and eGFR reference intervals; compare with baseline kidney function when available.
items.diagnostic_thresholds: Low eGFR, rising creatinine, oliguria, solitary kidney, obstruction concern, or sepsis physiology should trigger renal-dose and AKI-context review.
items.interpretation_cautions: Creatinine can lag behind acute kidney injury and must be interpreted with baseline kidney function, hydration status, muscle mass, pregnancy, medications, urine output, and local laboratory reporting.
items.retrieval_tags: dysuria; pyelonephritis; AKI; renal_function; reference_threshold; antibiotic_dosing
items.reviewer_notes: Source-backed demonstration reference-threshold item for staging workflow.

items.label: Urosepsis or obstructed infected stone escalation cues
items.action: Escalate urgently when dysuria or flank pain is accompanied by hypotension, altered mental status, rigors, persistent vomiting, oliguria, pregnancy, immunocompromise, or severe colicky flank pain with obstruction concern.
items.rationale: Systemic illness or obstruction concern changes a urinary presentation from isolated lower-tract symptoms into a higher-acuity evaluation where cultures, imaging, parenteral therapy, sepsis care, or urgent urologic involvement may be needed.
items.diagnostic_target: Sepsis physiology, obstructing infected stone, complicated UTI, or high-risk pyelonephritis
items.when_to_use_structured: dysuria; fever; rigors; flank_pain; vomiting; hypotension; altered_mental_status; oliguria; pregnancy_possible; immunocompromised; obstruction_concern
items.result_changes_management: Positive escalation cues change urgency, culture and imaging threshold, empiric therapy breadth, consult threshold, and disposition.
items.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this red-flag escalation rule; use the cited guideline context and patient acuity to identify high-risk complicated UTI presentations.
items.limitations: Absence of these cues does not exclude pyelonephritis or early sepsis; reassess trajectory, local resistance risk, pregnancy status, catheter status, immune status, obstruction risk, and available diagnostics.
items.retrieval_tags: dysuria; pyelonephritis; complicated_UTI; sepsis; nephrolithiasis; obstruction_concern; red_flag
items.reviewer_notes: Source-backed demonstration red-flag item for staging workflow.

items.label: Escalate dysuria workup when systemic or high-risk features are present
items.action: Treat fever, rigors, vomiting, pregnancy, sepsis physiology, immunocompromise, or obstructing-stone concern as a higher-risk presentation requiring escalation.
items.rationale: Systemic illness, pregnancy, vomiting, immunocompromise, or obstruction concern changes the presentation away from isolated lower urinary symptoms and can require broader testing, culture follow-up, imaging, parenteral therapy, or higher-acuity disposition.
items.diagnostic_target: Complicated UTI, pyelonephritis, sepsis, pregnancy-associated infection risk, or obstructing stone
items.when_to_use_structured: dysuria; fever; rigors; vomiting; pregnancy_possible; sepsis; flank_pain
items.result_changes_management: High-risk features change urgency, culture/imaging threshold, parenteral therapy consideration, and disposition.
items.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to this management rule; use the cited guideline and patient acuity to decide escalation.
items.limitations: This rule supports escalation framing and does not replace clinician judgment, local antibiogram review, pregnancy assessment, or sepsis evaluation.
items.reviewer_notes: Source-backed demonstration management-change item for staging workflow.

gold_cases.presentation: Burning pee with fever and flank pain
gold_cases.expected_history_labels: Ask about flank pain and fever
gold_cases.expected_test_labels: Urinalysis with urine culture when complicated infection features are present; Renal function threshold for antibiotic dosing and imaging safety
gold_cases.expected_red_flag_labels: Urosepsis or obstructed infected stone escalation cues
gold_cases.expected_management_change_labels: Escalate dysuria workup when systemic or high-risk features are present

suppress_rules.when_context_lacks: weakness; numbness; seizure; syncope; altered mental status
suppress_rules.reason: Avoid unrelated broad neurologic maneuvers for isolated urinary symptoms.
